steps.title: Identify the contrasting relationship
steps.content: The passage presents a contrast: the drummer “did not play drums on the classic song,” yet “joined the label afterward.” The word “however” must mark this contrast between two independent clauses—”He did not play drums...” and “he joined the label....

steps.title: Apply the punctuation rule for conjunctive adverbs
steps.content:
Two independent clauses connected by the conjunctive adverb “however” require proper punctuation. A establishes a clear sentence boundary with “however. He,” effectively separating the two independent clauses into distinct sentences.

B is grammatically malformed; C creates a comma splice (joining independent clauses with only a comma); D leaves the clauses running together without adequate punctuation.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
